speaker: CAESAR
text: Nor heaven nor Earth have been at peace tonight.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Thrice hath Calphurnia in her sleep cried out

speaker: CAESAR
text: “Help ho, they murder Caesar!”—Who’s within?

speaker: SERVANT
text: My lord.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Go bid the priests do present sacrifice,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And bring me their opinions of success.

speaker: SERVANT
text: I will, my lord.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: What mean you, Caesar? Think you to walk forth?

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: You shall not stir out of your house today.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Caesar shall forth. The things that threatened me

speaker: CAESAR
text: Ne’er looked but on my back. When they shall see

speaker: CAESAR
text: The face of Caesar, they are vanishèd.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Caesar, I never stood on ceremonies,

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Yet now they fright me. There is one within,

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Besides the things that we have heard and seen,

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Recounts most horrid sights seen by the watch.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: A lioness hath whelpèd in the streets,

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: And graves have yawned and yielded up their dead.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Fierce fiery warriors fought upon the clouds

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: In ranks and squadrons and right form of war,

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Which drizzled blood upon the Capitol.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: The noise of battle hurtled in the air,

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Horses did neigh, and dying men did groan,

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: And ghosts did shriek and squeal about the streets.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: O Caesar, these things are beyond all use,

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: And I do fear them.

speaker: CAESAR
text: What can be avoided

speaker: CAESAR
text: Whose end is purposed by the mighty gods?

speaker: CAESAR
text: Yet Caesar shall go forth, for these predictions

speaker: CAESAR
text: Are to the world in general as to Caesar.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: When beggars die there are no comets seen;

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: The heavens themselves blaze forth the death of princes.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Cowards die many times before their deaths;

speaker: CAESAR
text: The valiant never taste of death but once.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Of all the wonders that I yet have heard,

speaker: CAESAR
text: It seems to me most strange that men should fear,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Seeing that death, a necessary end,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Will come when it will come.

speaker: CAESAR
text: What say the augurers?

speaker: SERVANT
text: They would not have you to stir forth today.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Plucking the entrails of an offering forth,

speaker: SERVANT
text: They could not find a heart within the beast.

speaker: CAESAR
text: The gods do this in shame of cowardice.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Caesar should be a beast without a heart

speaker: CAESAR
text: If he should stay at home today for fear.

speaker: CAESAR
text: No, Caesar shall not. Danger knows full well

speaker: CAESAR
text: That Caesar is more dangerous than he.

speaker: CAESAR
text: We are two lions littered in one day,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And I the elder and more terrible.

speaker: CAESAR
text: And Caesar shall go forth.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Alas, my lord,

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Your wisdom is consumed in confidence.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Do not go forth today. Call it my fear

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: That keeps you in the house, and not your own.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: We’ll send Mark Antony to the Senate House,

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: And he shall say you are not well today.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Let me, upon my knee, prevail in this.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Mark Antony shall say I am not well,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And for thy humor I will stay at home.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Here’s Decius Brutus; he shall tell them so.

speaker: DECIUS
text: Caesar, all hail! Good morrow, worthy Caesar.

speaker: DECIUS
text: I come to fetch you to the Senate House.

speaker: CAESAR
text: And you are come in very happy time

speaker: CAESAR
text: To bear my greeting to the Senators

speaker: CAESAR
text: And tell them that I will not come today.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Cannot is false, and that I dare not, falser.

speaker: CAESAR
text: I will not come today. Tell them so, Decius.

speaker: CALPHURNIA
text: Say he is sick.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Shall Caesar send a lie?

speaker: CAESAR
text: Have I in conquest stretched mine arm so far,

speaker: CAESAR
text: To be afeard to tell graybeards the truth?

speaker: CAESAR
text: Decius, go tell them Caesar will not come.

speaker: DECIUS
text: Most mighty Caesar, let me know some cause,

speaker: DECIUS
text: Lest I be laughed at when I tell them so.

speaker: CAESAR
text: The cause is in my will. I will not come.

speaker: CAESAR
text: That is enough to satisfy the Senate.

speaker: CAESAR
text: But for your private satisfaction,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Because I love you, I will let you know.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Calphurnia here, my wife, stays me at home.

speaker: CAESAR
text: She dreamt tonight she saw my statue,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Which, like a fountain with an hundred spouts,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Did run pure blood; and many lusty Romans

speaker: CAESAR
text: Came smiling and did bathe their hands in it.

speaker: CAESAR
text: And these does she apply for warnings and portents

speaker: CAESAR
text: And evils imminent, and on her knee

speaker: CAESAR
text: Hath begged that I will stay at home today.

speaker: DECIUS
text: This dream is all amiss interpreted.

speaker: DECIUS
text: It was a vision fair and fortunate.

speaker: DECIUS
text: Your statue spouting blood in many pipes,

speaker: DECIUS
text: In which so many smiling Romans bathed,

speaker: DECIUS
text: Signifies that from you great Rome shall suck

speaker: DECIUS
text: Reviving blood, and that great men shall press

speaker: DECIUS
text: For tinctures, stains, relics, and cognizance.

speaker: DECIUS
text: This by Calphurnia’s dream is signified.

speaker: CAESAR
text: And this way have you well expounded it.

speaker: DECIUS
text: I have, when you have heard what I can say.

speaker: DECIUS
text: And know it now: the Senate have concluded

speaker: DECIUS
text: To give this day a crown to mighty Caesar.

speaker: DECIUS
text: If you shall send them word you will not come,

speaker: DECIUS
text: Their minds may change. Besides, it were a mock

speaker: DECIUS
text: Apt to be rendered, for someone to say

speaker: DECIUS
text: “Break up the Senate till another time,

speaker: DECIUS
text: When Caesar’s wife shall meet with better dreams.”

speaker: DECIUS
text: If Caesar hide himself, shall they not whisper

speaker: DECIUS
text: “Lo, Caesar is afraid”?

speaker: DECIUS
text: Pardon me, Caesar, for my dear dear love

speaker: DECIUS
text: To your proceeding bids me tell you this,

speaker: DECIUS
text: And reason to my love is liable.

speaker: CAESAR
text: How foolish do your fears seem now, Calphurnia!

speaker: CAESAR
text: I am ashamèd I did yield to them.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Give me my robe, for I will go.

speaker: CAESAR
text: And look where Publius is come to fetch me.

speaker: PUBLIUS
text: Good morrow, Caesar.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Welcome, Publius.—

speaker: CAESAR
text: What, Brutus, are you stirred so early too?—

speaker: CAESAR
text: Good morrow, Casca.—Caius Ligarius,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Caesar was ne’er so much your enemy

speaker: CAESAR
text: As that same ague which hath made you lean.—

speaker: CAESAR
text: What is ’t o’clock?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Caesar, ’tis strucken eight.

speaker: CAESAR
text: I thank you for your pains and courtesy.

speaker: CAESAR
text: See, Antony that revels long a-nights

speaker: CAESAR
text: Is notwithstanding up.—Good morrow, Antony.

speaker: ANTONY
text: So to most noble Caesar.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Bid them prepare within.—

speaker: CAESAR
text: I am to blame to be thus waited for.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Now, Cinna.—Now, Metellus.—What, Trebonius,

speaker: CAESAR
text: I have an hour’s talk in store for you.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Remember that you call on me today;

speaker: CAESAR
text: Be near me that I may remember you.

speaker: TREBONIUS
text:
Caesar, I will.
                 
                Aside
                .
               And so near will I be

speaker: TREBONIUS
text: That your best friends shall wish I had been further.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Good friends, go in and taste some wine with me,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And we, like friends, will straightway go together.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That every like is not the same, O Caesar,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The heart of Brutus earns to think upon.
